---
title: "SEM"
output: html_document
---

STRUCTURAL EQUATION MODELLING


```{r}
results <- read.csv(here("data", "clean data", "results.csv"), colClasses = c(sex = "character"))

#make maternal ID a factor
results$maternalID <- as.factor(results$maternalID)

#remove ambient, because these need to be modelled separately. also remove larval treatment of cool and mean, because the sample size isn't big enough to model - just not enough clutches.
#I think I will model the ambient larva treatment separately.
results_filtered <- results %>%
  filter(
    larval_treatment %in% c("warm", "hot"),
    pupal_treatment != "ambient") %>%
  
  mutate(numerical_pupal_treatment = case_when(
    pupal_treatment == "cool"  ~ 10.07,
    pupal_treatment == "mean"  ~  12.08,
    pupal_treatment == "warm"  ~  14.08,
    pupal_treatment == "hot"   ~  15.54,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )) %>% 
  mutate(numerical_larval_treatment = case_when(
    larval_treatment == "warm"  ~  12.76,
    larval_treatment == "hot"   ~  13.89,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg = weight * 1000) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg_centered = weight_mg - mean(weight_mg, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(across(
    c(weight_mg, egg_mean_1A, clutch_size),
    ~ as.numeric(scale(.)),
    .names = "{.col}_z"
  ))


results_filtered <- results_filtered %>%
  mutate(
    larval_treatment = factor(larval_treatment, levels = c("warm","hot")),
    pupal_treatment = factor(pupal_treatment, levels = c("cool","mean","warm","hot"))
  )

```


```{r}
#library(lavaan)

path_model <- "
  clutch_size ~ numerical_larval_treatment
  clutch_size ~ a*weight_mg
  weight_mg ~ b*numerical_larval_treatment
  indirect:=a*b
"

path_estimate <- sem(path_model, data = results_filtered, se = "bootstrap")

summary(path_estimate, ci = TRUE)
#ok this comes out with significant indirect effects but it doesn't use the negative binomial distribution, which is important when I have zeros and overdispersed count data
#another option is piecewise SEM with negative binomial distribution

AIC(path_estimate)


#library(semPlot)

#semPaths(path_estimate,
         #what = "std",          # standardized estimates
        # layout = "tree",
         #edge.label.cex = 1.2,
         #sizeMan = 8,
        # nCharNodes = 0,
         #residuals = FALSE)
```

```{r}
#try piecewise SEM
#library(piecewiseSEM) 

#remove zeros from the dataset because I can't do a ZINB or a hurdle model in PSEM
results_filtered <- results_filtered %>%
 filter(clutch_size != 0) %>%
  filter(!is.na(egg_mean_1A)) 


m_model <- lm(weight_mg ~ numerical_larval_treatment, data = results_filtered)

y_model <- glm.nb(clutch_size ~ numerical_larval_treatment + weight_mg,  data = results_filtered)



sem_model <- psem(m_model, y_model)

summary(sem_model)

plot(sem_model, show = "std", digits = 2)


coefs(sem_model, standardize.type = "menard.OE")


coefs(sem_model, standardize = "range")
 
 
```








```{r}
#model without correlated error between egg size and number
m_model <- lm(weight_mg ~ numerical_larval_treatment + numerical_pupal_treatment, data = results_filtered)

y_model <- glm.nb(clutch_size ~ numerical_larval_treatment + numerical_pupal_treatment + weight_mg,  data = results_filtered)

x_model <- lmer(egg_mean_1A ~ numerical_pupal_treatment + numerical_larval_treatment + weight_mg + clutch_size + (1|maternalID), data = results_filtered)

sem_model <- psem(m_model, y_model, x_model)



summary(sem_model, standardize = "scale")

plot(sem_model, show = "std", digits = 2)
```




```{r}
#unstandardised model

# Models
m_model <- lm(weight_mg ~ numerical_larval_treatment + numerical_pupal_treatment,
              data = results_filtered)

y_model <- glm.nb(clutch_size ~ numerical_larval_treatment + numerical_pupal_treatment + weight_mg,
                  data = results_filtered)

x_model <- lmer(egg_mean_1A ~ numerical_pupal_treatment + numerical_larval_treatment + weight_mg + clutch_size + (1|maternalID), data = results_filtered)

# SEM with correlated error between clutch size and egg size
sem_model <- psem(
  m_model,
  y_model,
  x_model,
  clutch_size %~~% egg_mean_1A   # this adds the correlated residual
)

# Summary with standardized coefficients
summary(sem_model) #dispalys coefficients in z-scores

# Plot standardized SEM
plot(sem_model, show = "unstd", digits = 2)
```


```{r}
#standardised model
# Models
m_model <- lm(weight_mg_z ~ numerical_larval_treatment + numerical_pupal_treatment,
              data = results_filtered)

y_model <- lm(clutch_size_z ~ numerical_larval_treatment + numerical_pupal_treatment + weight_mg_z,
                  data = results_filtered)

x_model <- lmer(egg_mean_1A_z ~ numerical_pupal_treatment + numerical_larval_treatment + weight_mg_z + clutch_size_z + (1|maternalID), data = results_filtered)

# SEM with correlated error between clutch size and egg size
sem_model <- psem(
  m_model,
  y_model,
  x_model,
  clutch_size %~~% egg_mean_1A   # this adds the correlated residual
)

# Summary with standardized coefficients
summary(sem_model,  conserve = TRUE) #dispalys coefficients in z-scores

# Plot standardized SEM
plot(sem_model, show = "std", digits = 2)

```
